blocks.en: A Wi-Fi channel is not one big tone — it is hundreds of narrow subcarriers spaced closely together. Spreading data across many subcarriers at once is called OFDM (Orthogonal Frequency-Division Multiplexing), and it is what makes Wi-Fi robust against echoes and interference.
blocks.zh: Wi-Fi 信道并非一个大音调，而是数百个紧密排列的窄子载波。把数据同时铺在众多子载波上，称为 OFDM（正交频分复用），它正是让 Wi-Fi 能抵抗回波与干扰的原因。

blocks.en: OFDM: one device uses ALL the subcarriers for its turn. OFDMA adds the ‘MA’ — Multiple Access — by handing different groups of subcarriers to different devices at the same time.
blocks.zh: OFDM：一台设备在其发送机会内使用“全部”子载波。OFDMA 加上了“MA”——多址——把不同的子载波组同时分给不同的设备。

blocks.en: Those groups are called Resource Units (RUs). A 20 MHz channel holds 242 usable subcarriers (‘tones’); it can be one 242-tone RU for a single user, or split into smaller RUs — 106, 52, or 26 tones — for several users at once.
blocks.zh: 这些组称为资源单元（RU）。一个 20 MHz 信道含 242 个可用子载波（“tone”）；它可以是给单一用户的一个 242-tone RU，也可拆成更小的 RU——106、52 或 26 tone——同时服务多个用户。

blocks.en: OFDMA shines when many devices each have a little to say. Instead of each waiting for a full turn, the AP packs them into one transmission — cutting overhead and latency on a crowded network.
blocks.zh: 当许多设备各有少量数据要发时，OFDMA 最为出色。AP 把它们打包进一次发送，而非让每个都等一整次轮次——在拥挤网络上降低了开销与时延。

summary.en: Where this lives in the standard

blocks.en: OFDMA and the resource-unit/subcarrier layout for High Efficiency (HE / Wi-Fi 6) PPDUs are defined in clause 27.3 — see 27.3.1.2 (OFDMA) and 27.3.2.2 (resource unit, guard, and DC subcarriers).
blocks.zh: 高效率（HE / Wi-Fi 6）PPDU 的 OFDMA 与资源单元/子载波布局定义于第 27.3 节——参见 27.3.1.2（OFDMA）与 27.3.2.2（资源单元、保护与直流子载波）。
